This classification is based on its shell morphology and phylogenetic relationships with other clams in the same family. The Veneridae family consists of approximately 300 species of saltwater clams, many of which are found in marine environments worldwide. Amoria physodes is likely to be one of these species, but further research is needed to confirm its exact classification. Further study would provide more detailed information about its specific classification within the Veneridae family.
